news.title: Goa's town planning department to lock 82 lakh sq meters under no-development zones
news.para: Goa's TCP department is designating over 82.8 lakh sqm of land as non-developable, marking a significant move to protect ecologically sensitive areas. This initiative, driven by forest department recommendations, aims to preserve natural cover, sand dunes, orchards, and water bodies across Salcete, Mormugao, Sattari, and Pernem for future generations.

news.title: PMRDA likely to hand over Mahalunge-Maan town planning scheme to PMC for implementation
news.para: The stalled Mahalunge-Maan Town Planning scheme is finally progressing. The Pune Metropolitan Region Development Authority will transfer the project's execution to the Pune Municipal Corporation. This move follows the merger of villages into PMC limits. The scheme aims to develop a 250-hectare area near Hinjewadi IT park. It promises improved infrastructure and planned urban growth for the region.

news.title: Hubballi-Dharwad civic body extends 5% property tax rebate till May-end
news.para: The Hubballi-Dharwad Municipal Corporation has extended its 5% property tax rebate until the end of May due to lower-than-expected collections. The drive began late, prompting the extension to boost the Rs 235 crore target for 2026-27, with Rs 90 crore collected so far. GIS work will also be offered free in July and August.

news.title: Maharashtra govt to move SC against Bombay HC order on 254 acre Mira-Bhayander land: Revenue minister
news.para: Maharashtra is preparing to take its fight to the Supreme Court after a Bombay High Court ruling favored two builders' claims to 254.88 acres in Mira-Bhayander. With the land's worth in crores, the government maintains it holds rightful ownership. Revenue Minister Chandrashekhar Bawankule declared that they will vigorously defend their position, aiming to keep this critical asset in government hands.

news.title: Fresh 39A proposals seek conversion of over 25,000 sq meters in Bardez, Pernem
news.para: The Town and Country Planning department has proposed converting over 25,000 sqm of land across Goa, including natural cover and orchard areas, to settlement zones. Nine proposals, cleared by the TCP board, are now open for public objections. Major conversions include a 18,733 sqm plot in Bardez for KGL Realtors and 9,750 sqm in Pernem for IRAP Infra LLP.

news.title: Uncertainty over acquisition of over 500 acres land for New Nagpur
news.para: The New Nagpur Project faces a major hurdle due to escalating land acquisition costs. Originally estimated at Rs3,000 crore, the expense has now surpassed Rs4,000 crore. This surge may force authorities to reduce the project's land acquisition by nearly 500 to 600 acres. Farmers are urged to complete formalities to avoid project delays and potential loss of benefits.

news.title: Pune: 12 metro stations on Hinjewadi-Shivajinagar route likely to open by June-end
news.para: Pune's Hinjewadi-Shivajinagar Metro Line 3 is poised for a partial opening by June-end, with 12 stations from Maan to Balewadi expected to become operational.  Officials confirmed the project is 95.2% complete, awaiting safety clearances.  Full corridor operations, including Shivajinagar, are anticipated by October, pending resolution of technical delays.

news.title: Luxury tax on large houses in Kerala garners ₹157 crore in five years
news.para: Luxury tax on residential buildings generated Rs 157.62 crore for the state exchequer over five years, with FY2025-26 alone contributing Rs 67.66 crore. Increased revenue is attributed to a growing number of large houses and intensified tax identification efforts. The tax applies to houses exceeding a prescribed area limit, with exemptions for smaller homes and rebates for advance payments.

news.title: Hyderabad: Case against two firms for duping over 40 investors of ₹39 crore
news.para: Cyberabad EOW has registered a cheating case against directors and partners of two firms for allegedly duping over 40 investors of Rs 39 crore in a proposed villa project at Pocharam. Investors paid upfront for villas but construction never began, and statutory permissions were not secured, indicating fraudulent intent.

news.title: Amazon India arm leases 2.78 lakh sq ft warehousing space in Bengaluru’s Nelamangala
news.para: Amazon India has secured a significant warehousing space of nearly 2.78 lakh sq ft in Bengaluru's Nelamangala. This move highlights the ongoing need for large logistics facilities by e-commerce firms. The sub-lease, starting from January, will see rent commencement in March 2026.

news.title: Birla Corporation's profit up 14.8% to ₹294.8 crore in Q4 FY26
news.para: Birla Corporation Ltd announced a net profit of Rs 294.77 crore for the March quarter of FY26. Revenue from operations saw a marginal increase. The cement business reported higher sales volume, reaching a record high for a quarter. However, jute division faced challenges due to raw material shortages and price hikes.

news.title: Bombay HC dismisses tenants’ plea to add new builder to their redevelopment petition
news.para: Bombay High Court dismissed a plea by 52 tenants seeking to add a new developer to their redevelopment petition. The court imposed Rs 5 lakh costs on the tenants. Judges stated the application appeared financed by the proposed developer to enforce an unenforceable right. The court found the move an abuse of process and coercion.

news.title: Haryana launches digital building plan system
news.para: Haryana Chief Minister Nayab Singh Saini launched an online building plan approval system and initiated sending J-Forms to farmers via WhatsApp, despite election code restrictions. He also disbursed over Rs 2,115 crore to beneficiaries under various welfare schemes, including direct benefit transfers for social security pensions and scholarships.

news.title: Brigade Hotel Ventures to invest ₹1,000 crore in Karnataka; opens rebranded Kochi hotel
news.para: Brigade Hotel Ventures (BHVL) is set to invest approximately ₹1,000 crore in Karnataka over the next five years for new projects and existing asset modernization. The company also plans a broader expansion, aiming to add 1,700 rooms with a capital outlay of ₹3,600 crore.

news.title: Pune district administration to frame SOP for RMC plants in three weeks
news.para: Pune district administration will finalize a standard operating procedure (SOP) for the ready-mix concrete (RMC) industry within three weeks to address pollution and traffic congestion. Interim restrictions on RMC vehicle movement are in place, with unrestricted movement allowed on Wednesdays, Saturdays, and Sundays, and limited hours on other days. Mandatory nameplates and cameras will be installed on RMC transport vehicles.

news.title: Patna civic body grants ₹40 lakh rebate to early property tax payers
news.para: Patna Municipal Corporation offered Rs 40 lakh tax relief to over 35,000 property owners for early payments.  A 5% rebate is available until June 30.  Despite collecting Rs 11 crore, collections are down from last year.  PMC is urging taxpayers to pay, with defaulters facing 1.5% monthly interest from October.

news.title: Odisha leads India in PMAY-U 2.0 housing completions
news.para: Odisha leads the nation in completing houses under Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana-Urban (PMAY-U 2.0), consistently securing the top spot since March 2025. The state has achieved significant progress, with 2,008 houses completed and many more underway. This success is credited to strong coordination and swift execution, with officials aiming to finish remaining projects ahead of schedule.

news.title: SBI pitches for review of affordable home definition, cites jump in average loan size
news.para: SBI Chairman C S Setty has urged the government to redefine affordable housing, citing a significant rise in the average home loan size to Rs 51 lakh from Rs 35-40 lakh two years ago. He stated that increasing housing costs necessitate a review of what constitutes an affordable home, impacting policy incentives for lenders.

news.title: RBI imposes penalties on YES Bank, Hinduja Housing Finance
news.para: The Reserve Bank of India has levied a Rs 31.80 lakh penalty on YES Bank for failing to implement a system for using KYC Identifiers for customer relationships. This action follows a supervisory inspection of the bank's financial position as of March 31, 2025. Additionally, Hinduja Housing Finance Ltd was fined Rs 1.8 lakh for governance non-compliance.
